title: No message size limit on Broadcast payloads
description: Trigger: Any authenticated client sends a Broadcast with a very large payload (e.g., 100MB). Bad behavior: The relay fan-out (state.rs broadcast()) clones the RelayMessage for each connected client and sends it. A single malicious or buggy client can force the relay to allocate O(N * payload_size) bytes and consume O(N) network bandwidth where N is the number of connected clients. axum's WebSocket handler has no built-in message size limit. The relay has no check on payload.len() in ws.rs before routing. Fix: add a MAX_PAYLOAD_BYTES constant and reject Broadcast messages that exceed it with RelayMessage::Error.
notes: CONFIRMED from tungstenite WebSocketConfig::default(): max_message_size=Some(64<<20)=64MiB, max_frame_size=Some(16<<20)=16MiB. These are enforced by tungstenite before axum delivers the message. Axum also exposes max_message_size() and max_frame_size() builder methods on WebSocketUpgrade. So the relay is NOT completely unlimited -- messages >64MB are dropped at the tungstenite layer. However, 64MB is still large enough for broadcast amplification DoS at meaningful scale (64MB * N clients). The finding stands: the relay should set an explicit, much lower limit (e.g. 1MB) appropriate for a chat relay.
